blocks.content: He found her handwriting in a kitchen drawer.

A grocery list. Nothing significant — coffee beans, the pasta she preferred, oranges. Dated three weeks before she died. Her handwriting, the specific way she wrote her letters, the slight left-lean she'd never corrected.
He sat down on the kitchen floor and stayed there for a long time.

Maya came by most Tuesdays. She never called ahead — she just came, let herself in with the spare key, made tea, and sat with him without requiring him to perform anything. Sometimes they talked. Sometimes they watched the same corner of the room in silence.
He was grateful for the Tuesdays.
The Christmas tree was still up.

He knew he should take it down. It was the wrong season entirely. He left it up because it was the last one they had argued about — too tall, she had been right, he had lost cheerfully, the way he had always lost that argument — and he was not ready to be in a house that didn't have it.
Some nights he plugged them in just to have the light.
